---
title: "Statistics Studies in Greece"
author: "stesiam"
description: "Compiling a list of public statistics schools in Greece and comparing their key features based on their study guides."
freeze: false
link-external-newwindow: true
categories: [R, EDA]
image: uni_class.jpg
image-alt: "Empty university lecture hall with rows of wooden seats facing a stage that has a chalkboard, podium, and chairs. The room has light blue walls, ornate trim, and two exit doors on either side."
fig-cap-location: bottom
date: "2023-06-20"
toc: true
toc-location: left
citation: true
#df_print: paged
title-block-banner: true
nocite: '@*'
#csl: apa-6th-edition.csl
#bibliography: [packages.bib, references.bib]
lang: en
format: 
  html:
    freeze: false
    code-link: true
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: 
      source: https://github.com/stesiam/stesiam.github.io/blob/gh-pages/posts/statistics-studies-in-greece/index.qmd
execute:
  echo: true
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 80
---

## Introduction

In this article, I would like to gather all the scattered information available about Statistics departments in Greece. I will mostly analyze information that can be found online, as if I were filling out a university application form. However, in any case, I would strongly advise against basing your choice of university solely on this article. In this category, I simply include all departments related to Statistics. The departments I will compare are the following:

- [Department of Statistics](https://www.dept.aueb.gr/el/stat/content/%CF%84%CE%BC%CE%AE%CE%BC%CE%B1-%CF%83%CF%84%CE%B1%CF%84%CE%B9%CF%83%CF%84%CE%B9%CE%BA%CE%AE%CF%82) at the Athens University of Economics and Business (AUEB)
- [Department of Statistics and Insurance Science](https://www.unipi.gr/unipi/el/sta-home.html) at the University of Piraeus
- [Department of Statistics and Actuarial-Financial Mathematics](https://www.aegean.gr/departments/%CF%84%CE%BC%CE%AE%CE%BC%CE%B1-%CF%83%CF%84%CE%B1%CF%84%CE%B9%CF%83%CF%84%CE%B9%CE%BA%CE%AE%CF%82-%CE%BA%CE%B1%CE%B9-%CE%B1%CE%BD%CE%B1%CE%BB%CE%BF%CE%B3%CE%B9%CF%83%CF%84%CE%B9%CE%BA%CF%8E%CE%BD-%E2%80%93-%CF%87%CF%81%CE%B7%CE%BC%CE%B1%CF%84%CE%BF%CE%BF%CE%B9%CE%BA%CE%BF%CE%BD%CE%BF%CE%BC%CE%B9%CE%BA%CF%8E%CE%BD-%CE%BC%CE%B1%CE%B8%CE%B7%CE%BC%CE%B1%CF%84%CE%B9%CE%BA%CF%8E%CE%BD) at the University of the Aegean
- [Department of Statistics and Insurance Science](https://stat.uowm.gr/) at the University of Western Macedonia


```{r}
#| label: import_r_libs
#| title: "Importing R libraries"
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(readxl)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(sysfonts)
library(reactable)
library(showtext)
library(reactablefmtr)
library(highcharter)
library(here)
```

```{r}
#| label: further_settings
#| title: "Additional settings and font loading"
#| echo: false

sysfonts::font_add('fb', '../../_extensions/quarto-ext/fontawesome/assets/webfonts/fa-brands-400.ttf')
sysfonts::font_add('fs', '../../_extensions/quarto-ext/fontawesome/assets/webfonts/fa-solid-900.ttf')

showtext_auto()
showtext::showtext_opts(dpi = 300)
```


## Entry Score

One of the first criteria for many prospective students is the entry score — the minimum grade you need to have achieved in the Panhellenic Exams (Greece's national university entrance exams) to be admitted to the respective department, or alternatively, the score of the last admitted student given the number of available places. In my opinion, this indicator reflects how desirable a department is, rather than the quality of its academic program. Below, I gathered historical entry score data (from 2013 to 2022) from all four Statistics departments and put together a simple chart. It shows that historically the department with the highest entry score is AUEB's (with the sole exception of 2015). The Statistics department at the University of Piraeus comes in second in terms of popularity. Last in preference are the regional Statistics departments — the University of the Aegean and the University of Western Macedonia, based in Samos and Grevena respectively. The fact that they are located outside the main urban centers of the country likely makes them less attractive to the majority of applicants.

```{r}
#| label: import_vasi_eisagogis
#| title: "Entry score and positions data"
#| echo: false

tmimata_statistikis = data.frame(
  "Year" = rep(c(2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022),4),
  "University" = c(rep("AUEB",10), rep("UniPi",10), rep("Aegean",10), rep("UoWM",10)),
  "Theseis" = c(99, 112, 103, 99, 103, 104, 113, 102, 103, 101,
                  189, 202, 198, 190, 198, 198, 216, 194, 154, 152, 
                  144, 137, 163, 156, 162, 178, 198, 171, 179, 165,
                NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 268, 225, 316, 256),
  "Vasi" = c(12788, 13277, 13488, 13158, 13511, 13354, 13337, 13425, 13316, 13828,
               12666, 13270, 13795, 12703, 12543, 11967, 11710, 12100, 11673, 12460,
               9618, 10636, 10574, 10471, 10692, 8525, 5148, 3925, 7797, 8425,
               NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 4846, 3950, 7867, 7740)
)

tmimata_statistikis = tmimata_statistikis |>
  mutate(
    img = "images/UniPi.png"
    )
```



```{r}
#| label: fig-vaseis-eisagogis
#| title: "Chart: Entry scores for Statistics departments"
#| fig-cap: "Entry scores for Statistics departments (2013 – 2022)"

title_text = "<strong>Entry scores for Statistics departments</strong>"
subtitle_text = "Relatively stable and respectable entry scores for the Attica departments compared to the regional Statistics departments"
caption_text = "<b>Source:</b> Panhellenic Entry Scores<br><span style='font-family:fb;'>&#xf09b;</span> <b>stesiam</b>, 2024"

hchart(tmimata_statistikis, "line", hcaes(x = Year, y = Vasi, group = University)) |>
  hc_title(
    text = title_text,
    align = "left") |>
  hc_xAxis(title = "Year") |>
  hc_yAxis(title = "Entry score") |>
  hc_subtitle(text = subtitle_text, align = "left") |>
  hc_caption(text = caption_text) %>%
  hc_tooltip(shared = TRUE)
```


## Available Places

Every year, the Ministry of Education announces the available places for each department and each category of applicants. In this analysis, I focus on candidates from General High Schools, who make up the overwhelming majority of participants. At first glance, I notice a reversal compared to the entry score data. That is, departments with a relatively higher entry score offer fewer places, while more positions are available at departments with lower entry scores. To illustrate: the Statistics department at AUEB hovers around 100 available places, followed by the University of Piraeus, which has significantly reduced its places in recent years and has dropped to levels comparable to those of the University of the Aegean's Statistics department. A special case is the newly established department in Grevena at the University of Western Macedonia, which had a considerably large number of available places — possibly due to a lack of admitted students for this particular regional department.

```{r}
#| label: fig-theseis-eisagogis
#| title: "Chart: Available places in Statistics departments"
#| fig-cap: "Available places in Statistics departments (2013 – 2022)"

title_text = "<b>Available places in Statistics departments (General High Schools)</b>"
subtitle_text = "Available places for each Statistics department. Places at departments outside major urban centers tend to go unfilled."

caption_text = "<b>Source:</b> Panhellenic Entry Scores<br><span style='font-family:fb;'>&#xf09b;</span> <b>stesiam</b>, 2024"

hchart(tmimata_statistikis, "line", hcaes(x = Year, y = Theseis, group = University)) |>
  hc_title(
    text = title_text,
    align = "left") |>
  hc_xAxis(title = "Year") |>
  hc_yAxis(title = "Available places") |>
  hc_subtitle(text = subtitle_text, align = "left") %>%
  hc_tooltip(shared = TRUE)
```

Of course, the availability of places does not always translate into filling them. Many positions may go unclaimed due to low demand, a phenomenon most visible in departments outside urban centers. In recent years, there is an additional contributing factor: the Minimum Admission Score (MAS) has been institutionalized, preventing a department's entry score from falling below a set threshold (e.g., below 7 or 13 on a 20-point scale). This explains the sharp upward spike visible in the first chart for the University of the Aegean and the University of Western Macedonia. Since the MAS was introduced, unfilled places have become common in low-demand regional departments. As a result, the number of enrolled students can now differ significantly — not only between departments inside and outside major urban centers (e.g., University of the Aegean, University of Western Macedonia – Grevena), but also between the officially available places and the actual number of registered students.

```{r}
#| label: fig-theseis-kat-analogia
#| title: "Chart: Proportional distribution of available places"
#| fig-cap: "Proportional distribution of available places in Statistics departments (2013 – 2022)"

years <- unique(tmimata_statistikis$Year)

# Prepare series data
series_data <- tmimata_statistikis %>%
  group_by(University) %>%
  summarise(data = list(Theseis)) %>%
  mutate(name = University) %>%
  select(name, data) %>%
  list_parse()

highchart() %>%
    hc_chart(type = "area") %>%
    hc_title(text = "Distribution of available places in Statistics departments") %>%
    hc_subtitle(text = "The University of Piraeus Statistics department historically offered the most places relative to the national total. The newly established Grevena department now holds the largest share.") %>%
    hc_xAxis(categories = years, tickmarkPlacement = "on", title = list(enabled = FALSE)) %>%
    hc_yAxis(title = list(text = "Percentage (%)"), labels = list(format = "{value}%")) %>%
    hc_tooltip(shared = TRUE, pointFormat = "<span style='color:{series.color}'>{series.name}</span>: <b>{point.percentage:.1f}%</b> ({point.y:,.0f})<br/>") %>%
    hc_plotOptions(area = list(
        stacking = "percent",
        lineColor = "#ffffff",
        lineWidth = 1,
        marker = list(lineWidth = 1, lineColor = "#ffffff")
    )) %>%
    hc_add_series_list(series_data)
```


## Graduation Requirements

All four departments offer a 4-year program, and a student is considered a graduate upon earning 240 ECTS credits. The differences lie mainly in the total number of required courses and whether a dissertation is a prerequisite for graduation. It should be noted that the following information is based on the most recent course guides of the respective departments (2022–2023).

::: {.column-margin}
UniPi2011 refers to the curriculum of the University of Piraeus Statistics department that applied to students who enrolled between 2011 and 2016. Students admitted from 2017 onward follow the updated 2017 curriculum (UniPi2017).
:::

```{r}
#| label: insert_data_dept_chars
#| title: "Data: Department characteristics"
#| echo: FALSE

degree_requirements = data.frame(
  "University" = c("AUEB", "UniPi2011", "UniPi2017", "Aegean", "UoWM"),
  "Courses" = c(32, 47, 40, 34, 48),
  "Comp" = c(14, 31, 19, 18, 41),
  "Elective" = c(18, 16, 21, 16, 7),
  "Ptixiaki" = c("Yes", "No", "No", "Yes", "No"),
  "Images" = c("<img src='https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/el/thumb/2/2c/AUEBEMBLEM.png/250px-AUEBEMBLEM.png' height='20'/>",
               "<img src='https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/UNIPI_Emblem.png/250px-UNIPI_Emblem.png' height='20'/>",
               "<img src='https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/UNIPI_Emblem.png/250px-UNIPI_Emblem.png' height='20'/>",
               "<img src='https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/el/thumb/7/7d/%CE%A0%CE%B1%CE%BD%CE%B5%CF%80%CE%B9%CF%83%CF%84%CE%AE%CE%BC%CE%B9%CE%BF_%CE%91%CE%B9%CE%B3%CE%B1%CE%AF%CE%BF%CF%85_%28logo%29.svg/250px-%CE%A0%CE%B1%CE%BD%CE%B5%CF%80%CE%B9%CF%83%CF%84%CE%AE%CE%BC%CE%B9%CE%BF_%CE%91%CE%B9%CE%B3%CE%B1%CE%AF%CE%BF%CF%85_%28logo%29.svg.png' height='20'/>",
              "<img src='https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/UoWM.png/120px-UoWM.png' height='20'/>")) |>
dplyr::arrange(-Courses) |>
mutate(Electivepct = round((Elective/Courses)*100, digits = 1))
```

```{r}
#| collapse: true
#| label: fig-courses-to-graduate
#| title: "Chart: Courses required to graduate"
#| fig-cap: "Required courses for graduation, by department"

highchart() %>%
  hc_chart(type = "bar") %>%
  hc_title(text = "Course structure of Statistics departments") %>%
  hc_subtitle(text = "The number of courses required to obtain an undergraduate degree from public Statistics departments in Greece, broken down by compulsory and elective components.")  %>%
  hc_caption(text = "stesiam, 2025") %>%
  hc_xAxis(
       categories = degree_requirements$Images,
         labels = list(useHTML = TRUE),
         title = list(text = NULL)
   ) %>%
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text = "Courses required to graduate")) %>%
  hc_plotOptions(series = list(stacking = "normal")) %>%
  hc_add_series(name = "Elective", data = degree_requirements$Elective, stack = "courses") %>%
  hc_add_series(name = "Compulsory", data = degree_requirements$Comp, stack = "courses")
```


## Elective Courses

Above, we saw how many courses a student must pass in order to graduate from each department. But how much freedom does each department actually offer for students to tailor their studies to their own interests and research preferences? This is what I tried to explore by looking at the number of elective courses and the percentage they represent out of the total required courses.

```{r}
#| collapse: true
#| label: fig-elective-courses
#| title: "Chart: Percentage of elective courses"
#| fig-cap: "Elective courses as a percentage of total courses required for graduation"

degree_requirements = degree_requirements |>
  arrange(-Electivepct)

highchart() %>%
  hc_chart(type = "bar") %>%
  hc_title(text = "Curriculum flexibility") %>%
  hc_subtitle(text = "The number of elective courses as a percentage of the total courses required for graduation.")  %>%
  hc_caption(text = "stesiam, 2025") %>%
 hc_xAxis(
       categories = degree_requirements$Images,
         labels = list(useHTML = TRUE),
         title = list(text = NULL)
   ) %>%
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text = "Elective / Total courses (%)")) %>%
  hc_plotOptions(series = list(stacking = "normal")) %>%
  hc_add_series(name = "Elective", data = degree_requirements$Electivepct, stack = "courses")
```


## Delayed Graduation

The University of Piraeus has the highest rate of delayed graduation, with 7 out of 10 students being in their 7th year of study or beyond. The departments in Athens and Samos follow at a significant distance. The Grevena department is newly established and has no students in extended study periods yet. Across all Statistics departments, there is a common pattern: a notably high proportion of undergraduate students have exceeded the standard n+2 years of study, and in most cases they make up the majority of the enrolled undergraduate population.

```{r}
#| label: syndesmos_foitites_ana_etos
#| title: "Link to ELSTAT data on students by years of study"
#| echo: false

# url = "https://www.statistics.gr/el/statistics?..."
# download.file(url = url, destfile = "undergrads_by_year.xls")
```

```{r}
#| label: filter_students_over6y
#| title: "Filtering students who have exceeded 6 years"
#| message: false

data = read_excel(path = "../../assets/data/data_students_gr_unis.xls")
undergrads_by_year <- data %>%
  dplyr::select(c(3,4,25)) %>%
  setNames(c("Dept", "StudentsUnEq6", "studentsOv6")) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(Dept) %>%
  .[-c(1),] %>%
  mutate(across(c(StudentsUnEq6, studentsOv6), ~ as.integer(.))) %>%
  mutate(totalStudents = StudentsUnEq6 + studentsOv6,
         pctOv6 = studentsOv6 / totalStudents,
         nonPctOv6 = 1 - pctOv6) %>%
  arrange(-pctOv6) %>%
  dplyr::filter(str_detect(Dept, "ΣΤΑΤΙΣΤΙΚΗ"))
```

```{r}
#| collapse: true
#| label: fig-late-graduation
#| title: "Chart: Rate of delayed graduation"
#| fig-cap: "Bar chart of delayed graduation rates"

highchart() %>%
  hc_chart(type = "bar") %>%
  hc_title(text = "Percentage of students who exceeded n+2 years") %>%
  hc_subtitle(text = "The majority of Statistics undergraduates are in their 7th year of study or above.") %>%
  hc_xAxis(categories = undergrads_by_year$Dept) %>%
  hc_yAxis(title = list(text = "Student percentage")) %>%
  hc_plotOptions(series = list(stacking = "normal")) %>%
  hc_add_series(name = "Students up to 6th year", data = undergrads_by_year$nonPctOv6) %>%
  hc_add_series(name = "Students in 7th year or above", data = undergrads_by_year$pctOv6) %>%
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 1,
    title = list(text = "Student percentage"),
    plotLines = list(
      list(
        color = "orange",
        width = 2,
        value = 0.5,
        dashStyle = "ShortDash",
        label = list(
          y = 200,
          text = paste0("Average: ", round(mean(undergrads_by_year$pctOv6, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1), "%"),
          align = "left",
          style = list(color = "orange", fontWeight = "bold", fontSize = 10)
        )
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  hc_tooltip(valueDecimals = 2)
```


## Other Information

```{r}
#| label: insert_data_stats_deptII
#| title: "Manual data entry – Department characteristics II"
#| echo: FALSE

others = data.frame(
  Uni = c("AUEB", "UniPi2011","UniPi2017", "Aegean", "UoWM"),
  Diloseis = c("Yes", "No", "No", "No", "No"),
  Alysides = c("Yes", "No", "No", "No", "No"),
  Ptyxiaki = c("Optional", "No", "No", "Optional", "No"),
  Vathmos = c("Arithmetic mean", "Weighted mean", "Arithmetic mean", "Weighted mean", "Arithmetic mean")
)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-basic-tables
#| title: "Table: Key curriculum characteristics"
#| fig-cap: "Key curriculum characteristics by department"

reactable::reactable(others,
                     columns = list(
    Uni = colDef(name = "University"),
    Diloseis = colDef(name = "Course registration limit", style = function(value) {
      if (value == "No") {
        color <- "#008000"
      } else {
        color <- "#e00000"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),
    Alysides = colDef(name = "Course prerequisites",
                      style = function(value) {
      if (value == "No") {
        color <- "#008000"
      } else {
        color <- "#e00000"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),
    Ptyxiaki = colDef(name = "Dissertation",
                      style = function(value) {
      if (value == "No") {
        color <- "#008000"
      } else {
        color <- "#e8c010"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),
    Vathmos = colDef(name = "Grade calculation")
  ))
```


## Map

Finally, I thought it would be a good exercise to put my map-making skills in R to the test. I usually work with Shapefiles as a base layer (in this case the borders of Greece), but this time I decided to use the rnaturalearth package. It's worth noting that the rnaturalearthdata and rnaturalhires packages are also required.

```{r}
#| label: students_by_dept
#| title: "Number of students per Statistics department"

students_by_dept = undergrads_by_year |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(Dept, "ΣΤΑΤΙΣΤΙΚΗ")) %>%
  mutate(Short = c("UniPi", "AUEB", "Aegean", "Grevena"))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-mapping-stats-dept
#| title: "Mapping Statistics departments"
#| fig-cap: "Map of Greece showing the geographic location of all undergraduate Statistics departments in the country."

uni_coord = data.frame(
  uni = c("UniPi", "Aegean", "AUEB", "UoWM"),
  unilong = c("University of Piraeus", "University of the Aegean",
              "Athens University of Economics and Business",
              "University of Western Macedonia"),
  dept = c("Statistics and Insurance Science", "Statistics and Actuarial-Financial Mathematics", "Statistics", "Statistics"),
  studs = c(3207, 1035, 1203, 555),
  long = c(23.6529793, 26.5664138, 23.7300928, 21.4565181),
  lat = c(37.77, 39.0851185, 38.1940201, 40.1197471),
  img = c("https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/UNIPI_Emblem.png/250px-UNIPI_Emblem.png",
          "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/d/df/Aegean_University_logo.gif",
          "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/89/AUEB_EMBLEM.png/250px-AUEB_EMBLEM.png",
          "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/UoWM.png/120px-UoWM.png"),
  established = c(1938, 1984, 1920, 2003),
  dpt_est = c(1977, 2000, 1989, 2019),
  town = c("Piraeus", "Samos", "Athens", "Grevena")
)

uni_coord$symbol <- rep(
  "url(https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e4/Map_marker_icon_%E2%80%93_Nicolas_Mollet_%E2%80%93_Pin.svg)",
  4
)

raw <- paste(readLines("../../assets/js/gr-all.js", warn = FALSE), collapse = "")
json_str <- stringr::str_extract(raw, "(?<=\\= )\\{.*\\}")
gr_map <- jsonlite::fromJSON(json_str, simplifyVector = FALSE)

highchart(type = "map") %>%
  hc_add_series(
    mapData = gr_map,
    name = "GR",
    showInLegend = FALSE
  ) %>%
  hc_add_series(
    data = uni_coord,
    type = "mappoint",
    hcaes(lon = long, lat = lat, name = uni),
    name = "Departments",
    color = "brown4",
    marker = list(
      symbol = "url(https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/5081/5081368.png)",
      width = 20,
      height = 20
    )
  ) %>%
  hc_title(text = "<b>Statistics Departments in Greece</b>") %>%
  hc_subtitle(text = "Greece has four public undergraduate Statistics departments in total, all offering 4-year programs. They are based in Athens, Piraeus, Samos, and Grevena.") %>%
  hc_caption(text = "stesiam, 2025") |>
  hc_tooltip(
    useHTML = TRUE,
    formatter = JS("
      function() {
        return '<div style=\"max-width: 330px; min-width: 200px; white-space: normal;\">' +
          '<div style=\"text-align: center;\">' +
            '<b>' + this.point.unilong + '</b><br>(' + this.point.uni + ')</div>' +
          '<br>' +
          '<div style=\"text-align: center;\"><em>' + this.point.dept + '</em></div>' +
          '<br>' +
          '<img style=\"margin: auto; display:flex; justify-content: center;\" src=\"' + this.point.img + '\" width=\"40\" height=\"40\" style=\"border-radius:10px; margin-top:5px;\" /><br>' +
          '<b>City:</b> ' + this.point.town + '<br>' +
          '<b>University founded:</b> ' + this.point.established + '<br>' +
          '<b>Department founded:</b> ' + this.point.dpt_est + '<br>' +
          '<b>Undergraduates:</b> ' + this.point.studs +
        '</div>';
      }
    ")
  ) %>%
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE)
```


## Epilogue

My personal view is that the Statistics department at the University of the Aegean is a very attractive option. It presents itself (and I do want to stress this — it *presents itself*) as a serious department with a modern curriculum: fewer courses, balanced out by an equivalent workload. In my opinion, this department is underrated relative to its entry score. If I had the choice without having to consider financial constraints (rent, cost of living, etc.), it would be my first pick. Of course, as I mentioned at the beginning, this opinion is based exclusively on information I found online and simply compiled into an article. There are many other factors that play a very significant role — such as the quality of the teaching staff, the rigor of individual courses, adherence to academic regulations, facilities, and accessibility — which, unfortunately, you will only discover when it is too late.


## Acknowledgements {.appendix .unlisted}

Image by <a href="https://pixabay.com/users/wokandapix-614097/?utm_source=link-attribution&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=image&utm_content=2093746">WOKANDAPIX</a> from <a href="https://pixabay.com//?utm_source=link-attribution&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=image&utm_content=2093746">Pixabay</a>
